premise: A glossary-style educational short teaches the word epiphany by asking the viewer what it means, visualizing the concept as a growing network of repeated definitions, and ending on a bilingual example-driven definition card.
plot_type: dictionary-question to semantic-cloud to bilingual definition payoff

cast.description: A faceless black torso silhouette appears only in the middle scene as a neutral memory avatar. It is not a real person, influencer, or supporting character with identity details.

continuity_rules: Do not introduce a photoreal presenter or influencer. The piece is typography-led and any human form should stay anonymous and graphic.
Scene_001 must feel like a magnified dictionary or reference page with a yellow word highlight and a questioning tone.
Scene_002 must preserve the anonymous silhouette, central hero word, repeated small glossary entries, and a bottom memory-score footer.
Scene_003 should simplify into a clean black definition card with bilingual example lines and a high completion-score footer.
The vocabulary system stays consistent across scenes: the same word, pronunciation family, yellow-accented hierarchy, and educational memory-bar framing.
No extra characters, room backgrounds, product shots, or lifestyle inserts should be added.

supporting_character_policy.rationale: The only human-like form is an abstract silhouette used as a conceptual graphic in scene_002. No second person or narrative supporting cast is needed.

scene_arc.beat: Open with a question-led hook that isolates the word epiphany on a highlighted dictionary page.
Expand the meaning into a visual memory cloud of repeated definitions orbiting an anonymous silhouette.
Land on the explicit bilingual definition and usage example as the retention payoff.
